Natural Illusionist.description: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip.

Speak with Animals.description: You always have Speak with Animals prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.

background_data.description: You spent formative years in a scriptorium, a monastery dedicated to the preservation of knowledge, or a government agency, where you learned to write with a clear hand and produce finely written texts. Perhaps you scribed government documents or copied tomes of literature. You might have some skill as a writer of poetry, narrative, or scholarly research. Above all, you have a careful attention to detail, helping you avoid introducing mistakes to the documents you copy and create.
background_data.name: Scribe

class_data.description: Scholars defined by exhaustive study of magic's inner workings. Wizards master spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, and spectacular transformations. Their mightiest magic can change matter, call down meteors, or open portals between worlds.

1.Arcane Recovery: When you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
1.Ritual Adept: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.

Spellcasting.description: You have learned to cast spells through study of arcane magic. You know three Wizard cantrips (Light, Mage Hand, and Ray of Frost are recommended). Your spellbook starts with six level 1 Wizard spells (Detect Magic, Feather Fall, Mage Armor, Magic Missile, Sleep, and Thunderwave are recommended). You prepare spells from your spellbook and can change your prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest.

Subclass Feature.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard subclass.

Ability Score Improvement.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

Subclass Feature.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard subclass.

Ability Score Improvement.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

choices.name: spell_mastery
choices.restrictions: Must select one level 1 spell and one level 2 spell

Spell Mastery.description: Choose a level 1 and a level 2 spell in your spellbook that have a casting time of an action. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them at their lowest level without expending a spell slot. You can replace one of those spells with an eligible spell of the same level when you finish a Long Rest.

19.Epic Boon: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Spell Recall is recommended.

Scholar.description: Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion. You have Expertise in the chosen skill.

Signature Spells.description: Choose two level 3 spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, and you can cast each of them once at level 3 without expending a spell slot. When you do so, you can't cast them in this way again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Wizard Subclass.description: You gain a Wizard subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Wizard levels.

Ability Score Improvement.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

5.Memorize Spell: Whenever you finish a Short Rest, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1+ Wizard spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another level 1+ spell from the book.

Subclass Feature.description: You gain a feature from your Wizard subclass.

Ability Score Improvement.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

multiclassing.source_text: As a Multiclass Character: Gain the Hit Point Die from the Core Wizard Traits table. Gain the Wizard's level 1 features, which are listed in the Wizard Features table. See the multiclassing rules in multiclassing to determine your available spell slots.

class_data.name: Wizard

effects.display: Advantage on INT, WIS, and CHA saves

class.description: Choose one of the following skills in which you have proficiency: Arcana, History, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, or Religion. You have Expertise in the chosen skill.
class.name: Scholar

class.description: When you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than half your Wizard level (round up), and none of the slots can be level 6 or higher. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
class.name: Arcane Recovery

class.description: You can cast any spell as a Ritual if that spell has the Ritual tag and the spell is in your spellbook. You needn't have the spell prepared, but you must read from the book to cast a spell in this way.
class.name: Ritual Adept

class.description: You have learned to cast spells through study of arcane magic. You know three Wizard cantrips (Light, Mage Hand, and Ray of Frost are recommended). Your spellbook starts with six level 1 Wizard spells (Detect Magic, Feather Fall, Mage Armor, Magic Missile, Sleep, and Thunderwave are recommended). You prepare spells from your spellbook and can change your prepared spells when you finish a Long Rest.
class.name: Spellcasting

feats.description:
You have a knack for learning new things. You gain proficiency in any combination of three skills or tools of your choice.

Choose any three skills or tools.

You gain proficiency with your chosen skills or tools.

This can include any combination of skills and tool proficiencies.
feats.name: Skilled

lineage.description:
You always have Speak with Animals prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.

Spells Always Prepared: Speak with Animals
lineage.name: Speak with Animals

lineage.description: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip.
lineage.name: Natural Illusionist

species.description: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose Forest Gnome or Rock Gnome. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait.
species.name: Gnomish Lineage

species.description: You have Advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.
species.name: Gnomish Cunning

species.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.
species.name: Darkvision

subclass.description: Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
subclass.name: Evocation Savant

subclass.description: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on a saving throw against the cantrip, the target takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.
subclass.name: Potent Cantrip

lineage_data.description: Forest Gnomes are reclusive illusionists who make their homes in dense woodlands. Attuned to the wild folk who share their forests, they slip easily between the natural and the fey, weaving Minor Illusion to misdirect intruders and speaking with the animals around them.
lineage_data.name: Forest Gnome

Natural Illusionist.description: You know the Minor Illusion cantrip.

Speak with Animals.description: You always have Speak with Animals prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. You can also use any spell slots you have to cast the spell.

save_advantages.display: Advantage on INT, WIS, and CHA saves

species_data.description: Gnomes are magical folk created by gods of invention, illusions, and life underground. They are petite folk with big eyes and pointed ears, who live around 425 years. Many gnomes like the feeling of a roof over their head, even if that roof is nothing more than a hat.
species_data.name: Gnome

Darkvision.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Gnomish Cunning.description: You have Advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.

effects.display: Advantage on INT, WIS, and CHA saves

Gnomish Lineage.description: You are part of a lineage that grants you supernatural abilities. Choose Forest Gnome or Rock Gnome. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait.

cantrips.description: You create a bubbling glob of acid and hurl it. Choose one or two creatures you can see within range. If you choose two, they must be within 5 feet of each other. A target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 Acid damage. This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).
cantrips.name: Acid Splash

cantrips.description: A spectral, floating hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you cast this spell again. You can use your action to control the hand. You can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. You can move the hand up to 30 feet each time you use it. The hand can't attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.
cantrips.name: Mage Hand

cantrips.description: You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell ignites if it isn't being worn or carried. This spell's damage increases by 1d10 when you reach 5th level (2d10), 11th level (3d10), and 17th level (4d10).
cantrips.name: Fire Bolt

cantrips.description: You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again. If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.
cantrips.name: Minor Illusion

level_1.description: For the duration, you can sense the presence of magic nearby and learn its general nature.
level_1.name: Detect Magic

level_1.description: Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 3d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites any flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.
level_1.name: Burning Hands

level_1.components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 50+ GP)
level_1.description: You hurl an orb of energy at a target within range. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder for the type of orb you create, and then make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 damage of the chosen type. If you roll the same number on two or more of the d8s, the orb leaps to a different target of your choice within 30 feet of the target. Make an attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. The orb can't leap again unless you cast the spell with a level 2+ spell slot.
level_1.name: Chromatic Orb

level_1.components: V, S, M (a bell and silver wire)
level_1.description: You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the warded area. You can designate creatures that won't set off the alarm. You choose whether the alarm is audible (handbell sound for 10 seconds within 60 feet) or mental (a ping if you are within 1 mile, which awakens you if asleep).
level_1.name: Alarm

level_1.description: For the duration, you can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the Influence action's skill options with them. Most Beasts have little to say about topics that don't pertain to survival or companionship, but at minimum, a Beast can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever it has perceived within the past day.
level_1.name: Speak with Animals

A target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 Acid damage. This spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.
0.name: Minor Illusion

1.description: For the duration, you can sense the presence of magic nearby and learn its general nature.
1.name: Detect Magic

1.description: Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 3d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire ignites any flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.
1.name: Burning Hands

1.components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 50+ GP)
1.description: You hurl an orb of energy at a target within range. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 damage of the chosen type. If you roll the same number on two or more of the d8s, the orb leaps to a different target of your choice within 30 feet of the target. Make an attack roll against the new target, and make a new damage roll. The orb can't leap again unless you cast the spell with a level 2+ spell slot.

1.components: V, S, M (a bell and silver wire)
1.description: You set an alarm against intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot Cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the warded area. You can designate creatures that won't set off the alarm. You choose whether the alarm is audible (handbell sound for 10 seconds within 60 feet) or mental (a ping if you are within 1 mile, which awakens you if asleep).
1.name: Alarm

1.description: For the duration, you can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the Influence action's skill options with them. Most Beasts have little to say about topics that don't pertain to survival or companionship, but at minimum, a Beast can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever it has perceived within the past day.
1.name: Speak with Animals

2.description: You can blind or deafen a foe. Choose one creature that you can see within range to make a Constitution saving throw. If it fails, the target has the Blinded or Deafened condition (your choice) for the duration. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make a Constitution saving throw. On a success, the spell ends. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd.
2.name: Blindness/Deafness

2.description: You fill the air with spinning daggers in a cube 5 feet on each side, centered on a point you choose within range. A creature takes 4d4 Slashing damage when it enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there. At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 2d4 for each slot level above 2nd.
2.name: Cloud of Daggers

subclass_data.description: Your studies focus on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid.

10.Empowered Evocation: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

14.Overchannel: You can increase the power of your spells. When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1-5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it. The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity. Each time you use this feature again before finishing a Long Rest, the Necrotic damage per spell level increases by 1d12.

3.Evocation Savant: Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.
3.Potent Cantrip: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on a saving throw against the cantrip, the target takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

6.Sculpt Spells: You can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocations. When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 plus the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

subclass_data.name: Evoker
